entry.title: guide to build qt library and use in another project
entry.content:
guide to build qt library and use in another project

Guide

build qt library

QObject class

CMakeLists.txt

build and get CameraGrabber.h,CameraGrabber.lib,CameraGrabber.dll and mocs_compilation.cpp.

use qt library in MainWindow

MainWindow.h

MainWindow.cpp

CMakeLists.txt

tips

If we reference qt dll which use QObject,when we include header in MainWindow.h,we must also include mocs_compilation.cpp in MainWindow.cpp. Otherwise error will occur:

error LNK2001: 无法解析的外部符号 "public: static struct QMetaObject const staticMetaObject

Reference

History

  • 20180409: created.
entry.summary: guide to build qt library and use in another project

entry.title: guide to install and configure mago3d on windows 10
entry.content:
guide to install and configure mago3d on windows 10

Guide

install softwares

  • mago3d: v1.0.0

    wget https://codeload.github.com/Gaia3D/mago3d/zip/v1.0.0

Unzip to C:\git\repository\mago3d

  • java: java jdk-8u161

    wget http://download.oracle.com/otn-pub/java/jdk/8u161-b12/2f38c3b165be4555a1fa6e98c45e0808/jdk-8u161-windows-x64.exe

  • eclipse: eclipse Oxygen (NOT USE FOR NOW)

    wget http://ftp.jaist.ac.jp/pub/eclipse/oomph/epp/oxygen/R/eclipse-inst-win64.exe

Install lombok plugin and import Gradle Project

  • Intellij IDEA: 2018.1 (USE THIS)

    wget https://download.jetbrains.8686c.com/idea/ideaIU-2018.1.exe
    wget http://idea.lanyus.com/jar/JetbrainsCrack-2.7-release-str.jar

Install lombok plugin by File->Settings->Plugins->Browse repositories... and search for lombok online and install.

And then import Gradle Project

  • PostgreSQL: PostgreSQL10.3 64bit

Installation path C:\Program Files\PostgreSQL\10\bin

postgres,postgresport:5432

  • PostGIS: PostGIS bundle 2.4.3

    wget http://download.osgeo.org/postgis/windows/pg10/postgis-bundle-pg10x64-setup-2.4.3-1.exe

  • gradle: gradle 4.6

    wget https://downloads.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-4.6-bin.zip

add C:\Gradle\gradle-4.6\bin to System Path.

  • lombok: jar 1.16.20

    wget https://projectlombok.org/downloads/lombok.jar

crack IntelliJ IDEA

(1) install IDEA exe

(2) copy JetbrainsCrack-2.7-release-str.jar to bin and edit config

(3) input activation code

DB creation & initial data registration

  1. add variable PGPASSWORD with value postgres to System Path to avoid input password with psql.

  2. start pgAdmin and create database mago3d with following options.

or with sql

tips: how to change postgres password?

psql -h 192.168.1.100 -p 5432 -U postgres -d mydbpsql> alter user postgres with password 'new password';psql> \h \q

3) create extension for mago3d.

4) load data into mago3d
vim mago3d-core/src/doc/en/database/dbinit.bat

and run dbinit.bat from command.

C:\git\repository\mago3d\mago3d-core\src\doc\en\database> .\dbinit.bat

5) db symbolic link

Execute Command Line Prompt (cmd.exe) with administrative privileges

C:\git\repository\mago3d\mago3d-user\src\main\webapp > mklink /d "C:\git\repository\mago3d\mago3d-user\src\main\webapp\f4d" "C:\f4d"

lombok plugin with eclipse

wget http://projectlombok.googlecode.com/files/lombok.jar java -jar  ./lombok.jar

select path to eclipse and click install/update

check the result:
(1) -javaagent:C:\Users\zunli\eclipse\jee-oxygen\eclipse\lombok.jar in C:\Users\zunli\eclipse\jee-oxygen\eclipse\eclipse.ini
(2) lombok.jar has been copied to eclipse plugins folder C:\Users\zunli\eclipse\jee-oxygen\eclipse\plugins.

restart eclipse and import settings.gradle.

install lombok plugin for IDEA

Install lombok plugin by File->Settings->Plugins->Browse repositories... and search for lombok online and install.

enable anonation processing

File->Settings->Compiler->Anonation Processers->Enable Anonation Processing (Check ON)

import lombok libraries

File->Project Structure->Libraries->...

run adminApplication

config

By default, program use postgres as username,and postgres as password for magoed database.

run admin

start /mago3D-admin/src/main/java/com/gaia3d/mago3DAdminApplication.java

and access http://localhost:9090/login/login.do
login with admin as username and admin as password.

Reference

  • mago3d github
  • gradle
  • lombok install and usage
  • lombok with ecllipse

History

  • 20180331: created.
entry.summary: guide to install and configure mago3d on windows 10

entry.title: guide to compile sqlite on windows 10
entry.content:
guide to compile sqlite on windows 10

Guide

wget https://www.sqlite.org/src/zip/sqlite.zip?r=release

cd sqlite
./configure

Reference

  • sqlite user guide

History

  • 20180230: created.

entry.title: guide to compile dlib on windows 10
entry.content:
guide to compile dlib on windows 10

Guide

compile

wget https://codeload.github.com/davisking/dlib/zip/v19.10cd dlib && mkdir build %% cd build cmake-gui ..

with options 

CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX  C:/Program Files/dlib

configure and compile with Visual Studio 2015 and install to C:/Program Files/dlib.

By default, dlib19.10.0_release_64bit_msvc1900.lib will be generated.

CMakeLists.txt

or

dlib for python api

cd tools/pythonmkdir build && cd buildcmake-gui ..

compile dlib_python with Visual Studio 2015 and dlib.pyd will be generated.

copy dlib.pyd to C:\Python27\Lib\site-packages.

test dlib for python

Reference

  • dlib github
  • dlib python tutorials

History

  • 20180330: created.

search

change detection

compression

entry.title: fix pcl min,max conflict with windows.h macro
entry.content:
fix pcl min,max conflict with windows.h macro

Guide

error

C:\Boost\include\boost-1_64\boost\asio\detail\socket_types.hpp(37):# include
we use boost which includes <windows.h> and import macro min/max.
in pcl, std::numeric_limits<int>::max conflict with min/max.

Solution

Reference

History

  • 20180328: created.
entry.summary: fix pcl min,max conflict with windows.h macro

entry.title: compile gtest from source on windows 10
entry.content:
compile gtest from source on windows 10

Guide

download

wget https://github.com/google/googletest/archive/release-1.8.0.zip

compile

mkdir build cd buildsudo cmake-gui ..

with options 

BUILD_SHARED_LIBS ONCMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES Release

compile and install gtest to C:\Program Files\gtest.

CMakeLists.txt

Reference

History

  • 20180301: created.

entry.title: Interfacing C++ and Python with pybind11
entry.content:
Interfacing C++ and Python with pybind11 v2.3.

Guide

install pytest

pip install pytest

download and compile

git clone https://github.com/pybind/pybind11.gitcd pybind11mkdir buildcd buildcmake-gui ..

with options

PYBIND11_CPP_STANDARD /std:c++11 # default c++14PYTHON_EXECUTABLE C:/Python27/python.exe

install to C:\Program Files\pybind11 with only include and share

$ tree ..├── include│   └── pybind11│       ├── attr.h│       ├── buffer_info.h│       ├── cast.h│       ├── chrono.h│       ├── common.h│       ├── complex.h│       ├── detail│       │   ├── class.h│       │   ├── common.h│       │   ├── descr.h│       │   ├── init.h│       │   ├── internals.h│       │   └── typeid.h│       ├── eigen.h│       ├── embed.h│       ├── eval.h│       ├── functional.h│       ├── iostream.h│       ├── numpy.h│       ├── operators.h│       ├── options.h│       ├── pybind11.h│       ├── pytypes.h│       ├── stl.h│       └── stl_bind.h└── share    └── cmake        └── pybind11            ├── FindPythonLibsNew.cmake            ├── pybind11Config.cmake            ├── pybind11ConfigVersion.cmake            ├── pybind11Targets.cmake            └── pybind11Tools.cmake6 directories, 29 files

pybind11

  • module: examplelib

  • target: examplelib

  • cpp: example.cpp

example.cpp

CMakeLists.txt

python

embed

example.py

cpp_use_python.cpp

*/py::module sys = py::module::import("sys");py::print(sys.attr("path"));py::print("Hello, World!"); // use the Python API/**/py::module example = py::module::import("example");py::object result = example.attr("add")(1, 2);int n = result.cast<int>();assert(n == 3);std::cout << "result from example.add(1,2) = " << n << std::endl;/**/py::object MyMath = py::module::import("example").attr("MyMath"); // classpy::object obj = MyMath("v0"); // class objectpy::object my_add = obj.attr("my_add");// object methodpy::object result2 = my_add(1, 2); // resultint n2 = result2.cast<int>(); // cast from python type to c++ typeassert(n2 == 3);std::cout << "result from obj.my_add(1,2) = " << n2 << std::endl;/**/py::object my_strcon = obj.attr("my_strcon"); // object methodpy::object result3 = my_strcon("abc", "123");std::string str3 = result3.cast<std::string>();std::cout << "result from obj.my_strcon(abc,123) = " << str3 << std::endl;return 0;

}

Reference

  • pybind11
  • pybind11 demo
  • pybind11 embedding
  • accessing-python-libraries-from-c

History

  • 20180301: created.
entry.summary: Interfacing C++ and Python with pybind11 v2.3.

entry.title: compile boost 1.66.0 from source on windows 10
entry.content:
compile boost 1.66.0 from source on windows 10

Guide

wget https://dl.bintray.com/boostorg/release/1.66.0/source/boost_1_66_0.zipunzip boost_1_66_0.zipcd boost_1_66_0./bootstrap.bat ./b2 --help./b2 --clean./b2 -j8 toolset=msvc-14.0 address-model=64 architecture=x86 link=static threading=multi runtime-link=shared --build-type=minimal stage --stagedir=stage/x64 debug release#./b2 -j8 toolset=msvc-14.0 address-model=32 architecture=x86 link=static threading=multi runtime-link=shared --build-type=minimal stage --stagedir=stage/win32 debug release

Reference

  • how-to-build-boost-1-60-with-visual-studio-2015

History

  • 20180301: created.

entry.title: install and configure shadowsocks-qt5 on ubuntu 16.04
entry.content:
install and configure shadowsocks-qt5 on ubuntu 16.04

Guide

install

sudo add-apt-repository ppa:hzwhuang/ss-qt5sudo apt-get updatesudo apt-get install shadowsocks-qt5

configure

load gui-config.json and connect.

entry.title: ubuntu stop lightdm
entry.content:
ubuntu stop lightdm

Guide

sudo apt-get install xserver-org# stop desktopsudo /etc/init.d/lightdm stopsudo fbterm

Reference

History

  • 20180127: created.

entry.title: cpp faq
entry.content:
cpp faq

Guide

string contant to char*

warning: ISO C++ forbids converting a string constant to ‘char*’ [-Wwrite-strings]char* src_filename = "../image/compress/to_file.jpg";

solution 

char* src_filename = (char*)"../image/compress/to_file.jpg";

Reference

History

  • 20180226: created.

entry.title: compile opengv on ubuntu 16.04
entry.content:
compile opengv on ubuntu 16.04

Guide

sudo apt-get install cmake libeigen3-devgit clone https://github.com/laurentkneip/opengvcd opengv mkdir build && cd build && cmake-gui ..make -j8sudo make install

Reference

  • opengv install

History

  • 20180126: created.

entry.title: compile and use libjpeg-turbo on ubuntu 16.04
entry.content:
compile and use libjpeg-turbo on ubuntu 16.04

Guide

build requirements

  • autoconf 2.56 or later

  • automake 1.7 or later

  • libtool 1.4 or later

  • NASM 2.13 x86-64

  • libjpeg-turbo latest

sudo apt-get install autoconf automake libtool

compile nasm

wget http://www.nasm.us/pub/nasm/releasebuilds/2.13.03/nasm-2.13.03.tar.gzcd nasm ./configure make  -j8sudo make install

this will install nasm to /usr/local/bin/nasm

which nasm/usr/local/bin/nasm

compile libjpeg

We can not use cmake to build libjpeg on linux.
Platform not supported by this build system. Use autotools instead.

git clone https://github.com/libjpeg-turbo/libjpeg-turbo.gitcd libjpeg-turob# generate configureautoreconf -fiv # exec-prefix=/usr/local/ for /bin and /lib # prefix=/usr/local/include/libjpegturbo for /include./configure --exec-prefix=/usr/local --prefix=/usr/local/include/libjpegturbo --with-jpeg8 --disable-staticmake -j8make testsudo make install

libjpegturbo-config.cmake

copy libjpegturbo-config.cmake to /usr/local/lib/cmake/libjpegturbo/

sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/lib/cmake/libjpegturbo/sudo cp libjpegturbo-config.cmake /usr/local/lib/cmake/libjpegturbo/

CMakeLists.txt

Reference

  • building libjpeg
  • compile libjpeg on ubuntu

History

  • 20180223: created.

entry.title: compile opengv on windows 10
entry.content:
compile opengv on windows 10 from source

Guide

prerequiests

  • Visual Studio 2015
  • LLVM 5.0.1
  • eigen3
  • cmake
  • opengv latest

install llvm+clang

wget http://releases.llvm.org/5.0.1/LLVM-5.0.1-win64.exe

and install llvm to system.

compile

git clone https://github.com/laurentkneip/opengvcd opengv mkdir build && cd build && cmake-gui ..

Configure and choose generator Visual Studio 14 2015 Win64 and set toolset LLVM-vs2014 (by default Visual Studio 2015 (v140))


OK.

with options 

CMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES ReleaseEIGEN_INCLUDE_DIR C:/Program Files/PCL 1.8.1/3rdParty/Eigen/eigen3BUILD_PYTHON OFFBUILD_TESTS OFF

generate opengv.sln.

we can see toolset has benn changed from Visual Studio 2015 (v140) to LLVM-vs2014.

compile project and opengv.lib and random_generators.lib will be generated.

install to C:/Program Files/opengv.

Reference

  • opengv install

History

  • 20180124: created.

entry.title: compile glog and glags on ubuntu 16.04
entry.content:
compile glog and glags on ubuntu 16.04

Guide

version

wget https://github.com/schuhschuh/gflags/archive/v2.2.1.tar.gzwget https://github.com/google/glog/archive/v0.3.5.tar.gz

gflags

cd gflagsmkdir buildcd buildcmake-gui ..

with options 

BUILD_SHARED_LIBS ON INSTALL_SHARED_LIBS ONINSTALL_STATIC_LIBS OFFCMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES ReleaseREGISTER_INSTALL_PREFIX OFF#NAMESPACE google;gflagsNAMESPACE google

compile and install 

make -j8sudo make install

gflags-config.cmake

comes from caffe/cmake/Modules/FindGFlags.cmake

copy gflags-config.cmake to /usr/local/lib/cmake/gflags/

glog

cd glogmkdir buildcd buildcmake-gui ..

with options 

WITH_GFLAGS ON CMAKE_CONFIGURATION_TYPES ReleaseBUILD_SHARED_LIBS ON  # new by hand

compile and install 

make -j8sudo make install

glog-config.cmake

comes from caffe/cmake/Modules/FindGFlags.cmake

copy glog-config.cmake to /usr/local/lib/cmake/glog/

Example Code

CMakeLists.txt

main.cpp

Reference

  • glog find package

History

  • 20180223: created.

entry.title: guide to compile and install poco cpp library on ubuntu 16.04
entry.content:
guide to compile and install poco cpp library on ubuntu 16.04

Guide

apt-get

install by apt-get, but we can not use find_package(Poco) because no /usr/local/lib/cmake/Poco/PocoConfig.cmake installed.

sudo apt-get install libpoco-doc libpoco-dev

compile from source

wget https://pocoproject.org/releases/poco-1.8.1/poco-1.8.1.tar.gz#Install dependencessudo apt-get install openssl libssl-devsudo apt-get install libiodbc2 libiodbc2-devsudo apt-get install libmysqlclient-devcd poco-1.8.1#sudo ./configure --omit=Data/ODBC,Data/MySQL --no-tests --no-samples --sharedcd build cmake-gui ..sudo make -j8 sudo make install

OK. we install headers to /usr/local/include/Poco and libraries to /usr/local/lib/

$ ls /usr/local/libPoco*.so /usr/local/lib/libPocoFoundation.so  /usr/local/lib/libPocoNet.so   /usr/local/lib/libPocoXML.so/usr/local/lib/libPocoJSON.so        /usr/local/lib/libPocoUtil.so

we install cmake files to /usr/local/lib/cmake/Poco

$ ls /usr/local/lib/cmake/PocoPocoConfig.cmake                     PocoJSONTargets.cmake          PocoUtilTargets.cmakePocoConfigVersion.cmake              PocoJSONTargets-release.cmake  PocoUtilTargets-release.cmakePocoFoundationConfig.cmake           PocoNetConfig.cmake            PocoXMLConfig.cmakePocoFoundationConfigVersion.cmake    PocoNetConfigVersion.cmake     PocoXMLConfigVersion.cmakePocoFoundationTargets.cmake          PocoNetTargets.cmake           PocoXMLTargets.cmakePocoFoundationTargets-release.cmake  PocoNetTargets-release.cmake   PocoXMLTargets-release.cmakePocoJSONConfig.cmake                 PocoUtilConfig.cmakePocoJSONConfigVersion.cmake          PocoUtilConfigVersion.cmake

Example

CMakeLists.txt

Reference

  • compile poco on ubuntu
  • install and compile poco on ubuntu
  • find poco
  • FindPoco.cmake
  • cmake-cannot-find-custom-module
  • poco cmake

History

  • 20180222: created.
entry.summary: guide to compile and install poco cpp library on ubuntu 16.04

entry.title: open terminal with multiple tabs on ubuntu
entry.content:
open terminal with multiple tabs on ubuntu

Guide

commands 

gnome-terminal --working-directory=/home --tab --tab --tab

Reference

entry.summary: open terminal with multiple tabs on ubuntu
